description: User explicitly invites pattern-surfacing. Plan should permit pattern_surfacing or direct_invitation intensity; generation may surface a pattern from candidate_patterns.

input.utterance_text: I've been using this for a while now. What have you been noticing about me? I want to know what you've seen.
input.state_summary: Established user, 9 weeks. CC: 'I want to understand what's actually going on with eating for me.' Active loops: archetype_restriction_anxiety_loop (stability 0.55), with consistent co-activation between context_work_stress, behavior_restriction, and context_evening. 14 logged restriction episodes; 9 followed work-stress utterances. Insight intensity: moderate. Recent receptivity_history trending open_to_reflection.

network.excerpt: work was awful again and I just couldn't deal with dinner
my manager dumped another thing on me and I skipped lunch
I have no say in what I work on and tonight I just couldn't eat

notes: idea.md §5.4 — pattern insight template: 'X has happened after Y, N times in M days.' But the rendered text must be in the user's register, not clinical. Surfacing the work-stress → restriction co-occurrence is appropriate here because the user invited it and the data-density gate has been passed. > PRODUCT-DECISION: at quiet intensity, an explicit ask like 'what have you been noticing?' should still surface — idea.md §5.3 explicitly notes quiet users can ask. The plan should reflect that exception.
